## [00:00 - 01:06]

If X plus 9 equals 10, most people know that X equals 1. I could never see that X equaled 1. And what I would have to do is plug in every single number and then finally hit, oh, it's 1. So to me, that's how I approach life. That's how I approach entrepreneurship. I solve for the variable that is unknown by just plugging sometimes random things in there. Sometimes it's not even an educated guess. Earlier this year, it's like, okay, well, you're not happy. Well, let's read some books on how to have more joy in your life. Oh, let's take that plug it in. Let's try it to see if it works. Oh, that didn't work. Take it out. Try another one. I think sometimes people get so caught up in getting the right answer that they don't try a bunch of different numbers. They don't try a bunch of different solutions quickly. And that's something that these guys write about. It's don't get hung up on the failures or the errors. Just iterate, try new things and keep moving. And it's just this constant, let's not take things personally. Let's not dwell on these. This is just the nature of the world that we live in. There's so much gold in what you were just saying. And it's something that I've seen you do over and over again. You treat life like a scientist.
